name: Max Baer
abstract: Maximilian Adelbert Baer Sr. (February 11, 1909 – November 21, 1959) was an American professional boxer and the world heavyweight champion from June 14, 1934, to June 13, 1935.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The night before, there was a boxing match between Joe Louis and Max Baer and I took the risk to come over to New York that night and celebrate Joe's victory in a bar on 7th Avenue and 143rd Street after hearing the fight on the radio.

about_quotes.speaker: Clark Terry
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Max Baer in the context of an attack on the night Joe Louis beat him; the interviewee (Clark Terry) does not mention Max Baer or indicate any personal connection.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clark Terry interviewed by Joe Williams and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, May 19, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Max Baer, he wanted to be a playboy and so one night he didn't show up for the show and he was out with the girls, so Billy Rose fired him

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: So two or three times I did, I sparred with him two or three times a week.
